title: Input Actions, Bindings, Focus, and Keyboard Equivalence · Runtime Lens: Focus Order
summary: Canvas clicks are the only navigation method, global key bindings fire inside text fields, focus becomes invisible, and repeat events race state changes. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the focus order increment in remappable action and focus-navigation map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Provide logical focus order, programmatic focus placement, visible focus, escape paths, and focus restoration after dialogs.
Choose widget, class, toplevel, tag, and bindtag scope while preserving expected default behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-focus-order — Provide logical focus order, programmatic focus placement, visible focus, escape paths, and focus restoration after dialogs.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-input-focus-bindings-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-input-focus-bindings-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_input_focus_bindings_2(actions: int = 9, keyboard_equivalents: int = 9, focus_stops: int = 7, trapped_stops: int = 0, ambiguous_bindings: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if actions < 1 or keyboard_equivalents < 0:
        return False, keyboard_equivalents, "action-count"
    if keyboard_equivalents != actions:
        return False, keyboard_equivalents, "input-equivalence"
    if focus_stops < 1 or trapped_stops > 0:
        return False, trapped_stops, "focus-order"
    if ambiguous_bindings > 0:
        return False, ambiguous_bindings, "binding-scope"
    assert keyboard_equivalents == actions
    return True, keyboard_equivalents, "equivalent-input-map"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-focus-order-18wxsr
# Competency: maze-focus-order.
# Case 2942: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: event-loop work must remain cancellable.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def evidence_maze_focus_order_18wxsr(actions: int = 9, keyboard_equivalents: int = 9, focus_stops: int = 7, trapped_stops: int = 0, ambiguous_bindings: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_18wxsr = "18wxsr-rsxw81-2942"
    assert evidence_variant_18wxsr
    evidence_axes_18wxsr = (
        "grid18wxsr",
        "topology2942",
        "searchrsxw81",
        "event18wxsr2942",
        "access294218wxsr",
        "recoveryrsxw812942",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_18wxsr)) == 6
    if actions < 1 or keyboard_equivalents < 0:
        return False, keyboard_equivalents, "action-count"
    if keyboard_equivalents != actions:
        return False, keyboard_equivalents, "input-equivalence"
    if focus_stops < 1 or trapped_stops > 0:
        return False, trapped_stops, "focus-order"
    if ambiguous_bindings > 0:
        return False, ambiguous_bindings, "binding-scope"
    assert keyboard_equivalents == actions
    return True, keyboard_equivalents, "equivalent-input-map"

steps.why: Retrieving Focus Order through the concrete remappable action and focus-navigation map case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Focus Order model · Theory Focus Order, keep Focus Order connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Provide logical focus order, programmatic focus placement, visible focus, escape paths, and focus restoration after dialogs.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Focus Order model · Theory Focus Order, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Focus Order precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Focus Order model · Theory Focus Order, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 7734 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile named actions, keyboard equivalence, focus order, and binding scope; all graph claims need an independent witness; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Provide logical focus order, programmatic focus placement, visible focus, escape paths, and focus restoration after dialogs.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Focus Order model · Theory Focus Order, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Canvas keyboard control works whenever the mouse is over it.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-focus-order-18wxsr.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-focus-order-18wxsr(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_focus_order_18wxsr\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?actions < 1 or keyboard_equivalents < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?keyboard_equivalents != actions)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?focus_stops < 1 or trapped_stops > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?ambiguous_bindings > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
